channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Intervalley-Coupled Twisted Bilayer Graphene from Substrate Commensuration
item.description:
Author(s): Bo-Ting Chen, Michael G. Scheer, and Biao Lian

We show that intervalley coupling can be induced in twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) by aligning the bottom graphene layer with either of two types of commensurate insulating triangular Bravais lattice substrate. The intervalley coupling folds the $±K$ valleys of TBG to the $\mathrm{Γ}$-point and hybr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066401] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bo-Ting Chen, Michael G. Scheer, and Biao Lian

We show that intervalley coupling can be induced in twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) by aligning the bottom graphene layer with either of two types of commensurate insulating triangular Bravais lattice substrate. The intervalley coupling folds the ±K valleys of TBG to the Γ-point and hybridizes the or…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066401] Published Mon Feb 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Intervalley-Coupled Twisted Bilayer Graphene from Substrate Commensuration
item.dc:creator: Bo-Ting Chen, Michael G. Scheer, and Biao Lian
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spectral Similarity Masks Structural Diversity at Hydrophobic Water Interfaces
item.description:
Author(s): Yong Wang, Yifan Li, Linhan Du, Chunyi Zhang, Lorenzo Agosta, Marcos Calegari Andrade, Annabella Selloni, and Roberto Car

The air-water and graphene-water interfaces represent quintessential examples of the liquid-gas and liquid-solid boundaries, respectively. While the sum-frequency generation (SFG) spectra of these interfaces show similarities, a consensus on their signals and interpretations has yet to be reached. L…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056202] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yong Wang, Yifan Li, Linhan Du, Chunyi Zhang, Lorenzo Agosta, Marcos Calegari Andrade, Annabella Selloni, and Roberto Car

The air-water and graphene-water interfaces represent quintessential examples of the liquid-gas and liquid-solid boundaries, respectively. While the sum-frequency generation (SFG) spectra of these interfaces show similarities, a consensus on their signals and interpretations has yet to be reached. L…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056202] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Spectral Similarity Masks Structural Diversity at Hydrophobic Water Interfaces
item.dc:creator: Yong Wang, Yifan Li, Linhan Du, Chunyi Zhang, Lorenzo Agosta, Marcos Calegari Andrade, Annabella Selloni, and Roberto Car

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Optical Signatures of $−\frac{1}{3}$ Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall State in Twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Haiyang Pan, Shunshun Yang, Yuzhu Wang, Xiangbin Cai, Wei Wang, Yan Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Linlong Zhang, Youwen Liu, Bo Yang, and Weibo Gao

The discovery of fractional charge excitations in new platforms offers crucial insights into strongly correlated quantum phases. While a range of fractional quantum anomalous Hall (FQAH) states have recently been observed in two-dimensional twisted moire systems, the theoretically anticipated fillin…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056601] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haiyang Pan, Shunshun Yang, Yuzhu Wang, Xiangbin Cai, Wei Wang, Yan Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Linlong Zhang, Youwen Liu, Bo Yang, and Weibo Gao

The discovery of fractional charge excitations in new platforms offers crucial insights into strongly correlated quantum phases. While a range of fractional quantum anomalous Hall (FQAH) states have recently been observed in two-dimensional twisted moire systems, the theoretically anticipated fillin…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 056601] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Optical Signatures of $−\frac{1}{3}$ Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall State in Twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Haiyang Pan, Shunshun Yang, Yuzhu Wang, Xiangbin Cai, Wei Wang, Yan Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Linlong Zhang, Youwen Liu, Bo Yang, and Weibo Gao

item.title: Integrable Spin Chains in Twisted Maximally Supersymmetric Yang-Mills Theory
item.description:
Author(s): Tim Meier and Stijn J. van Tongeren

We study an angular dipole deformation of maximally supersymmetric Yang-Mills (SYM) theory that preserves its classical scale invariance. Two-point functions of suitable single trace operators, restricted to an invariant plane, are determined by scaling dimensions computable via an integrable spin c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 051601] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tim Meier and Stijn J. van Tongeren

We study an angular dipole deformation of maximally supersymmetric Yang-Mills (SYM) theory that preserves its classical scale invariance. Two-point functions of suitable single trace operators, restricted to an invariant plane, are determined by scaling dimensions computable via an integrable spin c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 051601] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Integrable Spin Chains in Twisted Maximally Supersymmetric Yang-Mills Theory
item.dc:creator: Tim Meier and Stijn J. van Tongeren
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Particles and Fields

item.title: Real-Space Switching of Local Moments Driven by Quantum Geometry in Correlated Graphene Heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Niklas Witt, Siheon Ryee, Lennart Klebl, Jennifer Cano, Giorgio Sangiovanni, and Tim O. Wehling

Hybridization-induced topological transition between Mott states leads to flat bands, providing an alternative to twisted bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046505] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Niklas Witt, Siheon Ryee, Lennart Klebl, Jennifer Cano, Giorgio Sangiovanni, and Tim O. Wehling

Hybridization-induced topological transition between Mott states leads to flat bands, providing an alternative to twisted bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046505] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Real-Space Switching of Local Moments Driven by Quantum Geometry in Correlated Graphene Heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Niklas Witt, Siheon Ryee, Lennart Klebl, Jennifer Cano, Giorgio Sangiovanni, and Tim O. Wehling
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
